text:
# Page 1^page=1
Name: Lisa A English | DOB: 7/25/1964 | MRN: 055656 | PCP: Jung Wook Kang Smith, MD | Legal Name: Lisa A English Progress Notes Signed Jun 23, 2026 PHYSICAL THERAPY Patient: Lisa A English Age: 61 y.o. DOB: 7/25/1964 Narrative Attempt. Pt adamantly declining all activity at this time, despite encouragement/education. Pt reporting "needing medications to settle," pt agreeable to PTA/COTA returning ~1300. Tx. Pt in bed upon arrival. Pt needing mod encouragement for participation in PTA/COTA cotx as earlier pt stating agreeable for sliding board transfer to chair then attempting standing with BUE support to bedrails. Pt with incredibly high anxiety, talking in circles, and minimally allowing therapists to get a word in. Pt very particular with placement of items in room. Pt with increased c/o nausea and SOB- SpO2 92% throughout tx as pt assessing on personal pulse-ox. Attempted safety education as pt wanting to complete sliding board transfer to L side, however pt adamantly declining repositioning Bipap d/t positioning of cords tangled in bedrail. Assistance given to donn socks. Pt fully elevates HOB. Assistance given to place sliding board. Pt adamantly refusing gait belt. Pt completed sliding board transfer to chair (to R side) with supervision/touch assist. Pt then refusing attempting standing, despite encouragement. Pt remained sitting in chair with alarm on and all needs in reach. Pt currently agreeable to attempt standing with bedrail support during next tx- requesting ~1400. RT entering room upon departure. Recommendations Therapy Recommendations: Pt would benefit from continued therapy at a facility level Barriers to Discharge: Does not have assist needed for safe mobility/balance, Unable to negotiate home entry/exit safely, High risk for hospital readmission, Decreased cognition/safety awareness Equipment Recommended: Rolling walker, Reacher, Sock aid, Long-handled sponge This discharge disposition is recommended by PT based on this assessment; however, ultimate discharge disposition will be decided collaboratively with patient, caregivers, and multidisciplinary team. Outcome Measure Progress Notes by Courtney M at 06/23/26 0928

# Page 2^page=2
MyChart® licensed from Epic Systems Corporation© 1999 - 2026 Basic Mobility Raw Score: 12 (06/23/26 1325) Basic Mobility T-Scale Score: 32.23 (06/23/26 1325) Plan Treatment/Interventions: Transfer training, Balance training, LE strengthening/ROM, Endurance training, Patient/Family education, Gait training, Bed mobility, Home exercise program, Safety education Progress: Slow progress, medical status limitations PT Frequency: 6x/week Prior Level of Function Permanent Residence: Home Home Layout: One level, Ramped entrance Home Equipment Available : Rolling Walker, Cane, Manual wheelchair, CPAP/BiPAP, Oxygen, Drop arm bedside commode (bariatric BSC, slide pad, slide board) Prior Device Use: Motorized wheelchair or scooter, Mechanical lift Self Care (New): Needed some help Indoor Mobility (Ambulation) (New): Needed some help Stairs (New): Not applicable Functional Cognition (New): Independent Who do you live with?: Adult Child (son) Transfers Rolling left and right: Supervision or touching assistance Lying to sitting on side of bed: Independent, Using bedrail, HOB elevated Sitting to lying: Up in chair after treatment Sit to Stand: Patient refused Chair/bed to chair transfer: Supervision or touching assistance Balance Sitting - Static: Supervision or touching assistance Sitting - Dynamic: Supervision or touching assistance (SBA/CGA sliding board transfer) Standing - Static: Unable to assess Standing - Dynamic: Unable to assess Courtney Marlow, PTA
